mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following a 0.5pp UNRATE rise over a 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during mid-cycle expansions are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration shows a positive or zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns over a 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained nonfarm payroll growth above 200,000 per month confirms robust economic momentum and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS exceeds 200k monthly average for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods following three consecutive months of PAYEMS above 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates top-down economic expansion with bottom-up consumer confirmation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days via earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 70% of instances following two consecutive months of INDPRO contraction over a 36-month walk-forward test.
